newProfile builds a Profile for the given configuration.\nfunc newProfile(ctx context.Context, cfg config.KubeSchedulerProfile, r frameworkruntime.Registry, recorderFact RecorderFactory,\n\topts ...frameworkruntime.Option) (framework.Framework, error) {\n\trecorder := recorderFact(cfg.SchedulerName)\n\topts = append(opts, frameworkruntime.WithEventRecorder(recorder))\n\treturn frameworkruntime.NewFramework(ctx, r, &cfg, opts...)\n}\n\n// Map holds frameworks indexed by scheduler name.\ntype Map map[string]framework.Framework\n\n// NewMap builds the frameworks given by the configuration, indexed by name.\nfunc NewMap(ctx context.Context, cfgs []config.KubeSchedulerProfile, r frameworkruntime.Registry, recorderFact RecorderFactory,\n\topts ...frameworkruntime.Option) (Map, error) {\n\tm := make(Map)\n\tv := cfgValidator{m: m}\n\n\tfor _, cfg := range cfgs {\n\t\tp, err := newProfile(ctx, cfg, r, recorderFact, opts...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating profile for scheduler name %s: %v\", cfg.SchedulerName, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := v.validate(cfg, p); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tm[cfg.SchedulerName] = p\n\t}\n\treturn m, nil\n}\n\n// HandlesSchedulerName returns whether a profile handles the given scheduler name.\nfunc (m Map) HandlesSchedulerName(name string) bool {\n\t_, ok := m[name]\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// Close closes all frameworks registered in this map.\nfunc (m Map) Close() error {\n\tvar errs []error\n\tfor name, f := range m {\n\t\terr := f.Close()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"framework %s failed to close: %w\", name, err))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn errors.Join(errs...)\n}\n\n// NewRecorderFactory returns a RecorderFactory for the broadcaster.\nfunc NewRecorderFactory(b events.EventBroadcaster) RecorderFactory {\n\treturn func(name string) events.EventRecorder {\n\t\treturn b.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, name)\n\t}\n}\n\ntype cfgValidator struct {\n\tm             Map\n\tqueueSort     string\n\tqueueSortArgs runtime.Object\n}\n\nfunc (v *cfgValidator) validate(cfg config.KubeSchedulerProfile, f framework.Framework) error {\n\tif len(f.ProfileName()) == 0 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"scheduler name is needed\")\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Plugins == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugins required for profile with scheduler name %q\", f.ProfileName())\n\t}\n\tif v.m[f.ProfileName()] != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"duplicate profile with scheduler name %q\", f.ProfileName())\n\t}\n\n\tqueueSort := f.ListPlugins().QueueSort.Enabled[0].Name\n\tvar queueSortArgs runtime.Object\n\tfor _, plCfg := range cfg.PluginConfig {\n\t\tif plCfg.Name == queueSort {\n\t\t\tqueueSortArgs = plCfg.Args\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(v.queueSort) == 0 {\n\t\tv.queueSort = queueSort\n\t\tv.queueSortArgs = queueSortArgs\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif v.queueSort != queueSort {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"different queue sort plugins for profile %q: %q, first: %q\", cfg.SchedulerName, queueSort, v.queueSort)\n\t}\n\tif !cmp.Equal(v.queueSortArgs, queueSortArgs) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"different queue sort plugin args for profile %q\", cfg.SchedulerName)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","numLines":130,"startLine":1,"totalLines":130}}}

In kube-scheduler, after\n    39→\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\n    40→\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\n    41→\t// the type we converted from. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\n    44→\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\n    45→\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\n    46→\tmetav1.TypeMeta\n    47→\n    48→\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\n    49→\tParallelism int32\n    50→\n    51→\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\n    52→\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\n    53→\n    54→\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\n    55→\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\n    56→\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\n    57→\n    58→\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\n    59→\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n    60→\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n    61→\n    62→\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n    63→\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n    64→\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n    65→\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n    66→\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n    67→\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n    68→\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n    69→\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\n    70→\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n    71→\n    72→\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\n    73→\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\n    74→\t// will be used.\n    75→\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\n    76→\n    77→\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\n    78→\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\n    79→\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\n    80→\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\n    81→\n    82→\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\n    83→\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\n    84→\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\n    85→\t// with the \"default-scheduler\" profile, if present here.\n    86→\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\n    87→\n    88→\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\n    89→\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n    90→\tExtenders []Extender\n    91→\n    92→\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n    93→\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n    94→\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n    95→\t// Defaults to false.\n    96→\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→// KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\n   100→type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\n   101→\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n   102→\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n   103→\t// is scheduled with this profile.\n   104→\tSchedulerName string\n   105→\n   106→\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n   107→\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n   108→\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n   109→\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n   110→\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n   111→\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n   112→\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n   113→\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n   114→\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n   115→\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n   116→\n   117→\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n   118→\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n   119→\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n   120→\t// should be disabled.\n   121→\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n   122→\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n   123→\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n   124→\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n   125→\tPlugins *Plugins\n   126→\n   127→\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n   128→\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n   129→\t// for that plugin.\n   130→\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\n   131→}\n   132→\n   133→// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n   134→// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n   135→// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n   136→// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n   137→// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\n   138→type Plugins struct {\n   139→\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n   140→\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n   141→\n   142→\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n   143→\tQueueSort PluginSet\n   144→\n   145→\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   146→\tPreFilter PluginSet\n   147→\n   148→\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n   149→\tFilter PluginSet\n   150→\n   151→\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n   152→\tPostFilter PluginSet\n   153→\n   154→\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n   155→\tPreScore PluginSet\n   156→\n   157→\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n   158→\tScore PluginSet\n   159→\n   160→\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n   161→\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n   162→\tReserve PluginSet\n   163→\n   164→\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n   165→\tPermit PluginSet\n   166→\n   167→\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n   168→\tPreBind PluginSet\n   169→\n   170→\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   171→\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n   172→\tBind PluginSet\n   173→\n   174→\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n   175→\tPostBind PluginSet\n   176→\n   177→\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n   178→\tMultiPoint PluginSet\n   179→}\n   180→\n   181→// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n   182→// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\n   183→type PluginSet struct {\n   184→\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n   185→\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n   186→\tEnabled []Plugin\n   187→\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n   188→\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n   189→\tDisabled []Plugin\n   190→}\n   191→\n   192→// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\n   193→type Plugin struct {\n   194→\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n   195→\tName string\n   196→\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n   197→\tWeight int32\n   198→}\n   199→\n   200→// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n   201→// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n   202→// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\n   203→type PluginConfig struct {\n   204→\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n   205→\tName string\n   206→\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n   207→\tArgs runtime.Object\n   208→}\n   209→\n   210→/*\n   211→ * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\n   212→ */\n   213→const (\n   214→\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\n   215→\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\n   216→\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\n   217→\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\n   218→\n   219→\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\n   220→\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\n   221→\n   222→\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\n   223→\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n   224→\n   225→\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\n   226→\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\n   227→)\n   228→\n   229→// Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\n   230→func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\n   231→\tif p == nil {\n   232→\t\treturn nil\n   233→\t}\n   234→\textensions := []PluginSet{\n   235→\t\tp.PreEnqueue,\n   236→\t\tp.PreFilter,\n   237→\t\tp.Filter,\n   238→\t\tp.PostFilter,\n   239→\t\tp.Reserve,\n   240→\t\tp.PreScore,\n   241→\t\tp.Score,\n   242→\t\tp.PreBind,\n   243→\t\tp.Bind,\n   244→\t\tp.PostBind,\n   245→\t\tp.Permit,\n   246→\t\tp.QueueSort,\n   247→\t}\n   248→\tn := sets.New[string]()\n   249→\tfor _, e := range extensions {\n   250→\t\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\n   251→\t\t\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\n   252→\t\t}\n   253→\t}\n   254→\treturn sets.List(n)\n   255→}\n   256→\n   257→// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n   258→// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\n   259→type Extender struct {\n   260→\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n   261→\tURLPrefix string\n   262→\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n   263→\tFilterVerb string\n   264→\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n   265→\tPreemptVerb string\n   266→\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n   267→\tPrioritizeVerb string\n   268→\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n   269→\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n   270→\tWeight int64\n   271→\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n   272→\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n   273→\t// can implement this function.\n   274→\tBindVerb string\n   275→\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n   276→\tEnableHTTPS bool\n   277→\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n   278→\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\n   279→\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n   280→\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n   281→\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\n   282→\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n   283→\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n   284→\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n   285→\tNodeCacheCapable bool\n   286→\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n   287→\t// this extender.\n   288→\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n   289→\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n   290→\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n   291→\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n   292→\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n   293→\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n   294→\t// +optional\n   295→\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\n   296→\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n   297→\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n   298→\tIgnorable bool\n   299→}\n   300→\n   301→// ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n   302→// managed by an extender.\n   303→type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n   304→\t// Name is the extended resource name.\n   305→\tName string\n   306→\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n   307→\t// resource when applying predicates.\n   308→\tIgnoredByScheduler bool\n   309→}\n   310→\n   311→// ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\n   312→type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n   313→\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\n\t// with the \"default-scheduler\" profile, if present here.\n\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\n\n\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\n\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n\tExtenders []Extender\n\n\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n\t// Defaults to false.\n\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\n}\n\n// KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\ntype KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\n\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n\t// is scheduled with this profile.\n\tSchedulerName string\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n\n\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n\t// should be disabled.\n\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n\tPlugins *Plugins\n\n\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n\t// for that plugin.\n\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\n}\n\n// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\ntype Plugins struct {\n\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n\n\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n\tQueueSort PluginSet\n\n\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\tPreFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n\tFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n\tPostFilter PluginSet\n\n\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n\tPreScore PluginSet\n\n\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n\tScore PluginSet\n\n\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n\tReserve PluginSet\n\n\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n\tPermit PluginSet\n\n\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n\tPreBind PluginSet\n\n\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n\tBind PluginSet\n\n\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n\tPostBind PluginSet\n\n\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n\tMultiPoint PluginSet\n}\n\n// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\ntype PluginSet struct {\n\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n\tEnabled []Plugin\n\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n\tDisabled []Plugin\n}\n\n// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\ntype Plugin struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n\tName string\n\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n\tWeight int32\n}\n\n// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\ntype PluginConfig struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n\tName string\n\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n\tArgs runtime.Object\n}\n\n/*\n * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\n */\nconst (\n\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\n\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\n\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\n\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\n\n\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\n\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\n\n\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\n\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n\n\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\n\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\n)\n\n// Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\nfunc (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\n\tif p == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\textensions := []PluginSet{\n\t\tp.PreEnqueue,\n\t\tp.PreFilter,\n\t\tp.Filter,\n\t\tp.PostFilter,\n\t\tp.Reserve,\n\t\tp.PreScore,\n\t\tp.Score,\n\t\tp.PreBind,\n\t\tp.Bind,\n\t\tp.PostBind,\n\t\tp.Permit,\n\t\tp.QueueSort,\n\t}\n\tn := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, e := range extensions {\n\t\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\n\t\t\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sets.List(n)\n}\n\n// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\ntype Extender struct {\n\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n\tURLPrefix string\n\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n\tFilterVerb string\n\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n\tPreemptVerb string\n\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n\tPrioritizeVerb string\n\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n\tWeight int64\n\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n\t// can implement this function.\n\tBindVerb string\n\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n\tEnableHTTPS bool\n\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\n\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\n\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n\tNodeCacheCapable bool\n\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n\t// this extender.\n\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n\t// +optional\n\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\n\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n\tIgnorable bool\n}\n\n// ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n// managed by an extender.\ntype ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n\t// Name is the extended resource name.\n\tName string\n\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n\t// resource when applying predicates.\n\tIgnoredByScheduler bool\n}\n\n// ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\ntype ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\n\tInsecure bool\n\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\n\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\n\t// server is used.\n\tServerName string\n\n\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n\tCertFile string\n\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n\tKeyFile string\n\t// Trusted root certificates for server\n\tCAFile string\n\n\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\n\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\n\tCertData []byte\n\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\n\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\n\tKeyData []byte `datapolicy:\"security-key\"`\n\t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\n\t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\n\tCAData []byte\n}\n","numLines":337,"startLine":1,"totalLines":337}}}

runtime.Decoder) error {\n   102→\tvar strictDecodingErrs []error\n   103→\tfor i := range c.Profiles {\n   104→\t\tprof := &c.Profiles[i]\n   105→\t\tfor j := range prof.PluginConfig {\n   106→\t\t\terr := prof.PluginConfig[j].decodeNestedObjects(d)\n   107→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   108→\t\t\t\tdecodingErr := fmt.Errorf(\"decoding .profiles[%d].pluginConfig[%d]: %w\", i, j, err)\n   109→\t\t\t\tif runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\n   110→\t\t\t\t\tstrictDecodingErrs = append(strictDecodingErrs, decodingErr)\n   111→\t\t\t\t} else {\n   112→\t\t\t\t\treturn decodingErr\n   113→\t\t\t\t}\n   114→\t\t\t}\n   115→\t\t}\n   116→\t}\n   117→\tif len(strictDecodingErrs) > 0 {\n   118→\t\treturn runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(strictDecodingErrs)\n   119→\t}\n   120→\treturn nil\n   121→}\n   122→\n   123→// EncodeNestedObjects encodes plugin args.\n   124→func (c *KubeSchedulerConfiguration) EncodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\n   125→\tfor i := range c.Profiles {\n   126→\t\tprof := &c.Profiles[i]\n   127→\t\tfor j := range prof.PluginConfig {\n   128→\t\t\terr := prof.PluginConfig[j].encodeNestedObjects(e)\n   129→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\n   138→type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\n   139→\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n   140→\t// If 144→\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n   145→\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n   146→\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n   147→\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n   148→\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n   149→\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n   150→\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n   151→\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n   152→\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n   153→\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:\"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty\"`\n   154→\n   155→\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n   156→\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n   157→\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n   158→\t// should be disabled.\n   159→\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n   160→\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n   161→\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n   162→\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n   163→\tPlugins *Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n   164→\n   165→\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n   166→\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n   167→\t// for that plugin.\n   168→\t// +listType=map\n   169→\t// +listMapKey=name\n   170→\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig `json:\"pluginConfig,omitempty\"`\n   171→}\n   172→\n   173→// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n   174→// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n   175→// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n   176→// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n   177→// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\n   178→type Plugins struct {\n   179→\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n   180→\tPreEnqueue PluginSet `json:\"preEnqueue,omitempty\"`\n   181→\n   182→\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n   183→\tQueueSort PluginSet `json:\"queueSort,omitempty\"`\n   184→\n   185→\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   186→\tPreFilter PluginSet `json:\"preFilter,omitempty\"`\n   187→\n   188→\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n   189→\tFilter PluginSet `json:\"filter,omitempty\"`\n   190→\n   191→\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n   192→\tPostFilter PluginSet `json:\"postFilter,omitempty\"`\n   193→\n   194→\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n   195→\tPreScore PluginSet `json:\"preScore,omitempty\"`\n   196→\n   197→\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n   198→\tScore PluginSet `json:\"score,omitempty\"`\n   199→\n   200→\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n   201→\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n   202→\tReserve PluginSet `json:\"reserve,omitempty\"`\n   203→\n   204→\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n   205→\tPermit PluginSet `json:\"permit,omitempty\"`\n   206→\n   207→\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n   208→\tPreBind PluginSet `json:\"preBind,omitempty\"`\n   209→\n   210→\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   211→\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n   212→\tBind PluginSet `json:\"bind,omitempty\"`\n   213→\n   214→\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n   215→\tPostBind PluginSet `json:\"postBind,omitempty\"`\n   216→\n   217→\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config section to enable plugins for all valid extension points.\n   218→\t// Plugins enabled through MultiPoint will automatically register for every individual extension\n   219→\t// point the plugin has implemented. Disabling a plugin through MultiPoint disables that behavior.\n   220→\t// The same is true for disabling \"*\" through MultiPoint (no default plugins will be automatically registered).\n   221→\t// Plugins can still be disabled through their individual extension points.\n   222→\t//\n   223→\t// In terms of precedence, plugin config follows this basic hierarchy\n   224→\t//   1. Specific extension points\n   225→\t//   2. Explicitly configured MultiPoint plugins\n   226→\t//   3. The set of default plugins, as MultiPoint plugins\n   227→\t// This implies that a higher precedence plugin will run first and overwrite any settings within MultiPoint.\n   228→\t// Explicitly user-configured plugins also take a higher precedence over default plugins.\n   229→\t// Within this hierarchy, an Enabled setting takes precedence over Disabled. For example, if a plugin is\n   230→\t// set in both `multiPoint.Enabled` and `multiPoint.Disabled`, the plugin will be enabled. Similarly,\n   231→\t// including `multiPoint.Disabled = '*'` and `multiPoint.Enabled = pluginA` will still register that specific\n   232→\t// plugin through MultiPoint. This follows the same behavior as all other extension point configurations.\n   233→\tMultiPoint PluginSet `json:\"multiPoint,omitempty\"`\n   234→}\n   235→\n   236→// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n   237→// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\n   238→type PluginSet struct {\n   239→\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n   240→\t// If the default plugin is also configured in the scheduler config file, the weight of plugin will\n   241→\t// be overridden accordingly.\n   242→\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n   243→\t// +listType=atomic\n   244→\tEnabled []Plugin `json:\"enabled,omitempty\"`\n   245→\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n   246→\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n   247→\t// +listType=map\n   248→\t// +listMapKey=name\n   249→\tDisabled []Plugin `json:\"disabled,omitempty\"`\n   250→}\n   251→\n   252→// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\n   253→type Plugin struct {\n   254→\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n   255→\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n   256→\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n   257→\tWeight *int32 `json:\"weight,omitempty\"`\n   258→}\n   259→\n   260→// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n   261→// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n   262→// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\n   263→type PluginConfig struct {\n   264→\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n   265→\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n   266→\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n   267→\tArgs runtime.RawExtension `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n   268→}\n   269→\n   270→func (c *PluginConfig) decodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\n   271→\tgvk := SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(c.Name + \"Args\")\n   272→\t// dry-run to detect and skip out-of-tree plugin args.\n   273→\tif _, _, err := d.Decode(nil, &gvk, nil); runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(err) {\n   274→\t\treturn nil\n   275→\t}\n   276→\n   277→\tvar strictDecodingErr error\n   278→\tobj, parsedGvk, err := d.Decode(c.Args.Raw, &gvk, nil)\n   279→\tif err != nil {\n   280→\t\tdecodingArgsErr := fmt.Errorf(\"decoding args for plugin %s: %w\", c.Name, err)\n   281→\t\tif obj != nil && runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\n   282→\t\t\tstrictDecodingErr = runtime.NewStrictDecodingError([]error{decodingArgsErr})\n   283→\t\t} else {\n   284→\t\t\treturn decodingArgsErr\n   285→\t\t}\n   286→\t}\n   287→\tif parsedGvk.GroupKind() != gvk.GroupKind() {\n   288→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"args for plugin %s were not of type %s, got %s\", c.Name, gvk.GroupKind(), parsedGvk.GroupKind())\n   289→\t}\n   290→\tc.Args.Object = obj\n   291→\treturn strictDecodingErr\n   292→}\n   293→\n   294→func (c *PluginConfig) encodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\n   295→\tif c.Args.Object == nil {\n   296→\t\treturn nil\n   297→\t}\n   298→\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\n   299→\terr := e.Encode(c.Args.Object, &buf)\n   300→\tif err != nil {\n   301→\t\treturn err\n   302→\t}\n   303→\t// The <e> encoder might be a YAML encoder, but the parent encoder expects\n   304→\t// JSON output, so we convert YAML back to JSON.\n   305→\t// This is a no-op if <e> produces JSON.\n   306→\tjson, err := yaml.YAMLToJSON(buf.Bytes())\n   307→\tif err != nil {\n   308→\t\treturn err\n   309→\t}\n   310→\tc.Args.Raw = json\n   311→\treturn nil\n   312→}\n   313→\n   314→// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n   315→// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\n   316→type Extender struct {\n   317→\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n   318→\tURLPrefix string `json:\"urlPrefix\"`\n   319→\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n   320→\tFilterVerb string `json:\"filterVerb,omitempty\"`\n   321→\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n   322→\tPreemptVerb string `json:\"preemptVerb,omitempty\"`\n   323→\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n   324→\tPrioritizeVerb string `json:\"prioritizeVerb,omitempty\"`\n   325→\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n   326→\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n   327→\tWeight int64 `json:\"weight,omitempty\"`\n   328→\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n   329→\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n   330→\t// can implement this function.\n   331→\tBindVerb string `json:\"bindVerb,omitempty\"`\n   332→\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n   333→\tEnableHTTPS bool `json:\"enableHTTPS,omitempty\"`\n   334→\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n   335→\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig `json:\"tlsConfig,omitempty\"`\n   336→\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n   337→\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n   338→\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration `json:\"httpTimeout,omitempty\"`\n   339→\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n   340→\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n   341→\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n   342→\tNodeCacheCapable bool `json:\"nodeCacheCapable,omitempty\"`\n   343→\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n   344→\t// this extender.\n   345→\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n   346→\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n   347→\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n   348→\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n   349→\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n   350→\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n   351→\t// +optional\n   352→\t// +listType=atomic\n   353→\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource `json:\"managedResources,omitempty\"`\n   354→\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n   355→\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n   356→\tIgnorable bool `json:\"ignorable,omitempty\"`\n   357→}\n   358→\n   359→// ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n   360→// managed by an extender.\n   361→type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n   362→\t// Name is the extended resource name.\n   363→\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n   364→\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n   365→\t// resource when applying predicates.\n   366→\tIgnoredByScheduler bool `json:\"ignoredByScheduler,omitempty\"`\n   367→}\n   368→\n   369→// ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\n   370→type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n   371→\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\n   372→\tInsecure bool `json:\"insecure,omitempty\"`\n   373→\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\n   374→\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\n   375→\t// server is used.\n   376→\tServerName string `json:\"serverName,omitempty\"`\n   377→\n   378→\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n   379→\tCertFile string `json:\"certFile,omitempty\"`\n   380→\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n   381→\tKeyFile string `json:\"keyFile,omitempty\"`\n   382→\t// Trusted root certificates for server\n   383→\tCAFile string `json:\"caFile,omitempty\"`\n   384→\n   385→\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\n   386→\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\n   387→\t// +listType=atomic\n   388→\tCertData []byte `json:\"certData,omitempty\"`\n   389→\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\n   390→\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\n   391→\t// +listType=atomic\n   392→\tKeyData []byte `json:\"keyData,omitempty\"`\n   393→\t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\n   394→\t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\n   395→\t// +listType=atomic\n   396→\tCAData []byte `json:\"caData,omitempty\"`\n   397→}\n   398→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3e587527-d160-4a23-a9cf-89fb09df3295","uuid":"7b5a521d-d909-424f-881a-88d5a2f8d8ff","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/kube-scheduler/config/v1/types.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage v1\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\tcomponentbaseconfigv1alpha1 \"k8s.io/component-base/config/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/yaml\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// SchedulerDefaultLockObjectNamespace defines default scheduler lock object namespace (\"kube-system\")\n\tSchedulerDefaultLockObjectNamespace string = metav1.NamespaceSystem\n\n\t// SchedulerDefaultLockObjectName defines default scheduler lock object name (\"kube-scheduler\")\n\tSchedulerDefaultLockObjectName = \"kube-scheduler\"\n\n\t// SchedulerDefaultProviderName defines the default provider names\n\tSchedulerDefaultProviderName = \"DefaultProvider\"\n)\n\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n\n// KubeSchedulerConfiguration configures a scheduler\ntype KubeSchedulerConfiguration struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\n\tParallelism *int32 `json:\"parallelism,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\n\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfigv1alpha1.LeaderElectionConfiguration `json:\"leaderElection\"`\n\n\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\n\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\n\tClientConnection componentbaseconfigv1alpha1.ClientConnectionConfiguration `json:\"clientConnection\"`\n\n\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\n\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfigv1alpha1.DebuggingConfiguration\n\tcomponentbaseconfigv1alpha1.DebuggingConfiguration `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageofNodesToScore.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:\"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\n\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\n\t// will be used.\n\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds *int64 `json:\"podInitialBackoffSeconds,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\n\t// If specified, it must be greater than podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\n\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\n\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds *int64 `json:\"podMaxBackoffSeconds,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\n\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\n\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\n\t// with the \"default-scheduler\" profile, if present here.\n\t// +listType=map\n\t// +listMapKey=schedulerName\n\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile `json:\"profiles,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\n\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n\t// +listType=set\n\tExtenders []Extender `json:\"extenders,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n\t// Defaults to false.\n\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool `json:\"delayCacheUntilActive,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// DecodeNestedObjects decodes plugin args for known types.\nfunc (c *KubeSchedulerConfiguration) DecodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\n\tvar strictDecodingErrs []error\n\tfor i := range c.Profiles {\n\t\tprof := &c.Profiles[i]\n\t\tfor j := range prof.PluginConfig {\n\t\t\terr := prof.PluginConfig[j].decodeNestedObjects(d)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdecodingErr := fmt.Errorf(\"decoding .profiles[%d].pluginConfig[%d]: %w\", i, j, err)\n\t\t\t\tif runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\n\t\t\t\t\tstrictDecodingErrs = append(strictDecodingErrs, decodingErr)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn decodingErr\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(strictDecodingErrs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(strictDecodingErrs)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// EncodeNestedObjects encodes plugin args.\nfunc (c *KubeSchedulerConfiguration) EncodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\n\tfor i := range c.Profiles {\n\t\tprof := &c.Profiles[i]\n\t\tfor j := range prof.PluginConfig {\n\t\t\terr := prof.PluginConfig[j].encodeNestedObjects(e)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"encoding .profiles[%d].pluginConfig[%d]: %w\", i, j, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\ntype KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\n\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n\t// is scheduled with this profile.\n\tSchedulerName *string `json:\"schedulerName,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:\"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n\t// should be disabled.\n\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n\tPlugins *Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n\t// for that plugin.\n\t// +listType=map\n\t// +listMapKey=name\n\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig `json:\"pluginConfig,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\ntype Plugins struct {\n\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n\tPreEnqueue PluginSet `json:\"preEnqueue,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n\tQueueSort PluginSet `json:\"queueSort,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\tPreFilter PluginSet `json:\"preFilter,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n\tFilter PluginSet `json:\"filter,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n\tPostFilter PluginSet `json:\"postFilter,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n\tPreScore PluginSet `json:\"preScore,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n\tScore PluginSet `json:\"score,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n\tReserve PluginSet `json:\"reserve,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n\tPermit PluginSet `json:\"permit,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n\tPreBind PluginSet `json:\"preBind,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n\tBind PluginSet `json:\"bind,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n\tPostBind PluginSet `json:\"postBind,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config section to enable plugins for all valid extension points.\n\t// Plugins enabled through MultiPoint will automatically register for every individual extension\n\t// point the plugin has implemented. Disabling a plugin through MultiPoint disables that behavior.\n\t// The same is true for disabling \"*\" through MultiPoint (no default plugins will be automatically registered).\n\t// Plugins can still be disabled through their individual extension points.\n\t//\n\t// In terms of precedence, plugin config follows this basic hierarchy\n\t//   1. Specific extension points\n\t//   2. Explicitly configured MultiPoint plugins\n\t//   3. The set of default plugins, as MultiPoint plugins\n\t// This implies that a higher precedence plugin will run first and overwrite any settings within MultiPoint.\n\t// Explicitly user-configured plugins also take a higher precedence over default plugins.\n\t// Within this hierarchy, an Enabled setting takes precedence over Disabled. For example, if a plugin is\n\t// set in both `multiPoint.Enabled` and `multiPoint.Disabled`, the plugin will be enabled. Similarly,\n\t// including `multiPoint.Disabled = '*'` and `multiPoint.Enabled = pluginA` will still register that specific\n\t// plugin through MultiPoint. This follows the same behavior as all other extension point configurations.\n\tMultiPoint PluginSet `json:\"multiPoint,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\ntype PluginSet struct {\n\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n\t// If the default plugin is also configured in the scheduler config file, the weight of plugin will\n\t// be overridden accordingly.\n\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n\t// +listType=atomic\n\tEnabled []Plugin `json:\"enabled,omitempty\"`\n\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n\t// +listType=map\n\t// +listMapKey=name\n\tDisabled []Plugin `json:\"disabled,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\ntype Plugin struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n\tWeight *int32 `json:\"weight,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\ntype PluginConfig struct {\n\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n\tArgs runtime.RawExtension `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc (c *PluginConfig) decodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\n\tgvk := SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(c.Name + \"Args\")\n\t// dry-run to detect and skip out-of-tree plugin args.\n\tif _, _, err := d.Decode(nil, &gvk, nil); runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(err) {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar strictDecodingErr error\n\tobj, parsedGvk, err := d.Decode(c.Args.Raw, &gvk, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdecodingArgsErr := fmt.Errorf(\"decoding args for plugin %s: %w\", c.Name, err)\n\t\tif obj != nil && runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\n\t\t\tstrictDecodingErr = runtime.NewStrictDecodingError([]error{decodingArgsErr})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn decodingArgsErr\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif parsedGvk.GroupKind() != gvk.GroupKind() {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"args for plugin %s were not of type %s, got %s\", c.Name, gvk.GroupKind(), parsedGvk.GroupKind())\n\t}\n\tc.Args.Object = obj\n\treturn strictDecodingErr\n}\n\nfunc (c *PluginConfig) encodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\n\tif c.Args.Object == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\n\terr := e.Encode(c.Args.Object, &buf)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// The <e> encoder might be a YAML encoder, but the parent encoder expects\n\t// JSON output, so we convert YAML back to JSON.\n\t// This is a no-op if <e> produces JSON.\n\tjson, err := yaml.YAMLToJSON(buf.Bytes())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tc.Args.Raw = json\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\ntype Extender struct {\n\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n\tURLPrefix string `json:\"urlPrefix\"`\n\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n\tFilterVerb string `json:\"filterVerb,omitempty\"`\n\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n\tPreemptVerb string `json:\"preemptVerb,omitempty\"`\n\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n\tPrioritizeVerb string `json:\"prioritizeVerb,omitempty\"`\n\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n\tWeight int64 `json:\"weight,omitempty\"`\n\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n\t// can implement this function.\n\tBindVerb string `json:\"bindVerb,omitempty\"`\n\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n\tEnableHTTPS bool `json:\"enableHTTPS,omitempty\"`\n\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig `json:\"tlsConfig,omitempty\"`\n\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration `json:\"httpTimeout,omitempty\"`\n\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n\tNodeCacheCapable bool `json:\"nodeCacheCapable,omitempty\"`\n\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n\t// this extender.\n\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n\t// +optional\n\t// +listType=atomic\n\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource `json:\"managedResources,omitempty\"`\n\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n\tIgnorable bool `json:\"ignorable,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n// managed by an extender.\ntype ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n\t// Name is the extended resource name.\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n\t// resource when applying predicates.\n\tIgnoredByScheduler bool `json:\"ignoredByScheduler,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\ntype ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n    65→\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n    66→\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n    67→\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n    68→\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n    69→\t// nodes will be scored. When specified, the list of plugins for\n   134→// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n   135→// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n   136→// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n   137→// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\n   138→type Plugins struct {\n   139→\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n   140→\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n   141→\n   142→\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n   143→\tQueueSort PluginSet\n   144→\n   145→\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   146→\tPreFilter PluginSet\n   147→\n   148→\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n   149→\tFilter PluginSet\n   150→\n   151→\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n   152→\tPostFilter PluginSet\n   153→\n   154→\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n   155→\tPreScore PluginSet\n   156→\n   157→\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n   158→\tScore PluginSet\n   159→\n   160→\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n   161→\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n   162→\tReserve PluginSet\n   163→\n   164→\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n   172→\tBind PluginSet\n   173→\n   174→\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n   175→\tPostBind PluginSet\n   176→\n   177→\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n   178→\tMultiPoint PluginSet\n   179→}\n   180→\n   181→// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n   182→// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\n   183→type PluginSet struct {\n   184→\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n   185→\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n   186→\tEnabled []Plugin\n   187→\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n   188→\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n   189→\tDisabled []Plugin\n   190→}\n   191→\n   192→// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. HandlesSchedulerName(name string) bool {\n    69→\t_, ok := m[name]\n    70→\treturn ok\n    71→}\n    72→\n    73→// Close closes all frameworks registered in this map.\n    74→func (m Map) Close() error {\n    75→\tvar errs []error\n    76→\tfor name, f := range m {\n    77→\t\terr := f.Close()\n    78→\t\tif err != nil {\n    79→\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"framework %s failed to close: %w\", name, err))\n    80→\t\t}\n    81→\t}\n    82→\treturn errors.Join(errs...)\n    83→}\n    84→\n    85→// NewRecorderFactory returns a RecorderFactory for the broadcaster.\n    86→func NewRecorderFactory(b events.EventBroadcaster) RecorderFactory {\n    87→\treturn func(name string) events.EventRecorder {\n    88→\t\treturn b.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, name)\n    89→\t}\n    90→}\n    91→\n    92→type cfgValidator struct {\n    93→\tm             Map\n    94→\tqueueSort     string\n    95→\tqueueSortArgs runtime.Object\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→func (v *cfgValidator) validate(cfg config.KubeSchedulerProfile, f framework.Framework) error {\n    99→\tif len(f.ProfileName()) == 0 {\n   100→\t\treturn errors.New(\"scheduler name is needed\")\n   101→\t}\n   102→\tif cfg.Plugins == nil {\n   103→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"plugins required for profile with scheduler name %q\", f.ProfileName())\n   104→\t}\n   105→\tif v.m[f.ProfileName()] != nil {\n   106→\t\treturn runtime.Object\n   111→\tfor _, plCfg := range cfg.PluginConfig {\n   112→\t\tif plCfg.Name == queueSort {\n   113→\t\t\tqueueSortArgs = plCfg.Args\n   114→\t\t\tbreak\n   115→\t\t}\n   116→\t}\n   117→\tif len(v.queueSort) == 0 {\n   118→\t\tv.queueSort = queueSort\n   119→\t\tv.queueSortArgs = queueSortArgs\n   120→\t\treturn nil\n   121→\t}\n   122→\tif v.queueSort != queueSort {\n   123→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"different queue sort plugins for profile %q: %q, first: %q\", cfg.SchedulerName, queueSort, v.queueSort)\n   124→\t}\n   125→\tif !cmp.Equal(v.queueSortArgs, queueSortArgs) {\n   126→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"different queue sort plugin args for profile %q\", cfg.SchedulerName)\n   127→\t}\n   128→\treturn nil\n   129→}\n   130→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n    41→\n    42→// KubeSchedulerConfiguration configures a scheduler\n    43→type KubeSchedulerConfiguration struct {\n    44→\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n    45→\n    46→\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods.
